title: The square-ish phone that I wanted to love
summary: The Verge reviews the Ikko MindOne Pro, a compact phone with a square screen and innovative features including a flipping camera for selfies and optional keyboard accessory. Despite multiple attempts to make the device work—testing it as a standard phone, a minimalist launcher platform, and a dumb phone—the reviewer found it fundamentally disappointing. The clever design innovations cannot overcome deeper usability and functionality shortcomings.
content:
A great concept that falls flat.	

The Ikko MindOne Pro is delightfully small.
I keep calling it a square phone, which isn't quite right; the screen is square, but the phone itself is slightly rectangular. The camera flips up so you can use it for selfies - you can even open it partway to use as a stand or a kind of PopSocket. There's a Clicks-style keyboard accessory that also adds a magnetic ring and a headphone jack. I tried so hard to like it, but this phone is a miss no matter how you look at it.
I used it like a normal phone. I downloaded a minimalist launcher and tried using it as kind of a dumb phone. I put the keyboard case on. I took the keyboard case off. Nothing …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 我曾想爱上的那款方形手机
zh.summary: The Verge评测了Ikko MindOne Pro，一款屏幕为方形设计的紧凑型手机，具有创新功能，包括可翻转的自拍摄像头和可选的键盘配件。尽管评测者尝试了多种使用方式——将其当作标准手机、极简启动器平台和功能机——但最终发现这款手机根本令人失望。巧妙的设计创新无法弥补其更深层的可用性和功能缺陷。

title: The fanfiction community is at war with AI — and itself
summary: Fanfiction communities have initiated a movement to identify and remove AI-generated works, citing concerns about tools like Claude and ChatGPT. The detection methods being employed are unreliable and risk misidentifying human-written content as AI-generated, creating internal community conflict. The initiative highlights tension between creators' desire to preserve the community and the practical difficulty of reliably detecting AI-written submissions.
content:
Fanfiction communities are trying to hunt down writers who haven’t written works with their own hands. | Image: Álvaro Bernis / The Verge	

Over the past week, a new fanworks movement has kicked off, with the aim to root out authors using generative AI. But the detection methods being implemented are questionable, and any fanfic writer could be caught in the crossfire.
Broad distaste around the use of Claude, ChatGPT, and other AI tools has long been a thing in creative communities, including the world of fanfiction. Readers and writers have passed around tips for spotting supposedly AI-generated works, citing anything from em dashes to the broad concept of purple prose. But on June 29th, an anonymous X account called @heatedrivalryai promised a seemingly more reliable solution …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: 同人文社区与AI及自身的对抗
zh.summary: 同人文社区已启动一项运动，旨在识别和删除AI生成的作品，声称对Claude和ChatGPT等工具感到担忧。所采用的检测方法并不可靠，存在将人类创作的内容误判为AI生成的风险，引起社区内部冲突。这一举措凸显了创作者想要保护社区与实际难以可靠检测AI创作投稿之间的紧张关系。

title: When the ability to smell goes away
summary: Loss of smell is frequently associated with underlying brain health issues, suggesting olfactory disturbances may serve as an important medical warning sign. Researchers are investigating the connections between sensory disruptions and neurological conditions. Early detection of smell loss could help identify serious health problems before they progress.
content: Disturbances in this critical sense are often linked to problems with brain health.

zh.title: 当嗅觉消失时
zh.summary: 嗅觉丧失常常与潜在的脑部健康问题相关，这表明嗅觉障碍可能是一个重要的医学警示信号。研究人员正在调查感觉障碍与神经系统疾病之间的关联。早期发现嗅觉丧失可以帮助在严重健康问题恶化之前及时识别。

title: A martian rock has lots of carbon on it, and it's not clear why
summary: Scientists discovered a Martian rock with unusually high carbon content, raising questions about the sample's origins. Biological processes could theoretically explain the finding, but several other mechanisms may also be responsible. The discovery adds to the ongoing investigation of Mars' geology and whether conditions ever supported life.
content: Biology could explain the find, but there are other potential explanations.

zh.title: 火星岩石含有大量碳，原因不明
zh.summary: 科学家发现了一块含有异常高碳含量的火星岩石，这引发了人们对样本来源的疑问。从理论上讲，生物过程可以解释这一发现，但其他几种机制也可能是原因。这一发现为持续的火星地质学研究补充了新信息，也涉及火星是否曾经存在支持生命的条件这一问题。

title: Qi fan fan
summary:
The Kuxiu D5 keeps Qi-compatible phones cool while also charging a watch and earbuds.	

Despite my initial skepticism, I'm now sold on wireless Qi chargers that add integrated fans to keep your phone
content:
The Kuxiu D5 keeps Qi-compatible phones cool while also charging a watch and earbuds.	

Despite my initial skepticism, I'm now sold on wireless Qi chargers that add integrated fans to keep your phone cool while charging. I figured they'd be too loud, or too weak, or too gimmicky, but I'm a convert after spending a week with the new $59.99 Kuxiu D5 Qi2.2 charging dock. Its active cooling system has kept my phone from heating up, unlike every other Qi charger I've tried. Good thing, given my own history with an overheating phone.
Last year I fried the logic board on my titanium iPhone 15 Pro. I was on a sweltering train and trying to edit a 4K video on my phone, which was charging from a magnetically attached Qi power bank. Oof, …
Read the full story at The Verge.

zh.title: Qi冷却风扇
zh.summary: Kuxiu D5可以为Qi兼容手机散热，同时还能为手表和耳塞充电。尽管我最初持怀疑态度，但现在我已经爱上了这种内置风扇的无线Qi充电器。
